title: Antifungal Drugs
summary: الأدوية المضادة للفطريات تنقسم إلى مجموعتين رئيسيتين: أدوية للعدوى الفطرية العميقة (الجهازية) مثل Amphotericin B وFlucytosine والـAzoles والـEchinocandins، وأدوية للعدوى السطحية الجلدية مثل Terbinafine وNystatin. يعمل Amphotericin B بالارتباط بـErgosterol في غشاء الخلية الفطرية مما يسبب تسرب المحتويات الخلوية وموت الخلية. الـAzoles تعمل بتثبيط إنزيم P450 المسؤول عن تحويل Lanosterol إلى Ergosterol. Terbinafine هو الخيار الأول لعلاج الفطريات الجلدية خاصة فطريات الأظافر.

key_drugs.name: Amphotericin B
key_drugs.note: Drug of choice for life-threatening systemic mycoses; binds ergosterol

key_drugs.name: Flucytosine (5-FC)
key_drugs.note: Narrow spectrum; synergistic with Amphotericin B; causes bone marrow depression

key_drugs.name: Ketoconazole
key_drugs.note: First broad-spectrum oral antifungal; inhibits steroid synthesis; teratogenic

key_drugs.name: Fluconazole
key_drugs.note: Drug of choice for Cryptococcus and Candida; excellent CSF penetration

key_drugs.name: Voriconazole
key_drugs.note: Drug of choice for invasive aspergillosis; causes visual/auditory hallucinations

key_drugs.name: Caspofungin
key_drugs.note: Inhibits beta-glucan synthase; active against Aspergillus and Candida resistant to azoles

key_drugs.name: Terbinafine
key_drugs.note: Inhibits squalene epoxidase; drug of choice for onychomycosis

key_drugs.name: Nystatin
key_drugs.note: Topical only for oral candidiasis (thrush); too toxic for systemic use

key_drugs.name: Griseofulvin
key_drugs.note: For dermatophytes; now replaced by terbinafine

mnemonics.title: Ampho يحضن Ergosterol
mnemonics.body: تخيل ان Amphotericin B شخص اسمه 'امفو' بيحضن بقوة 'ارجو ستيرول' (Ergosterol) في غشاء الخلية الفطرية، فيخلي ثقوب (pores) تسرب منها كل محتويات الخلية فتموت. كلمة 'امفو' = Ampho، و'ارجو' = Ergosterol

mnemonics.title: FAVKI - Azoles الجهازية
mnemonics.body:
Fluconazole - Aspergillus (Voriconazole) - Ketoconazole - Itraconazole
F = Fluconazole (Crypto/Candida)
V = Voriconazole (invasive Aspergillosis)
K = Ketoconazole (broad but toxic)
I = Itraconazole (Aspergillus/Chromo)

mnemonics.title: 5-FC مصنع داخل الفطر
mnemonics.body: تخيل مصنعا داخل الخلية الفطرية فقط يحول Flucytosine (5-FC) الى 5-FU السام، ثم يتحول الى مادة تحطم DNA وRNA. المصنع لا يعمل في خلايا الانسان، لذا الدواء انتقائي للفطر.

mnemonics.title: Terbinafine يحرق السكوالين
mnemonics.body: Terbinafine = 'تيربي' يحرق (inhibits) squalene epoxidase فيتراكم السكوالين السام داخل الفطر ويقتله. يتراكم في الجلد والاظافر مثل الدهن، لذا هو الافضل لـ onychomycosis.

mnemonics.title: CRANE - اعراض Amphotericin B
mnemonics.body:
C = Convulsions (intrathecal)
R = Rigors & fever (immediate)
A = Anemia (normocytic normochromic)
N = Nephrotoxicity (dose-dependent, delayed)
E = Electrolyte loss (hypokalemia)

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Binds to Ergosterol in the fungal cell membrane, forming pores that cause leakage of ions and enzymes, leading to cell death (fungicidal).

flashcards.front: What is the drug of choice for cryptococcal meningitis?
flashcards.back: Amphotericin B (given intrathecally)

flashcards.front: How does Flucytosine (5-FC) work?
flashcards.back: Converted inside the fungal cell only to 5-FU, which then inhibits thymidylate synthase, blocking DNA and RNA synthesis.

flashcards.front: Why is Flucytosine used with Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Amphotericin B increases cell membrane permeability, allowing larger amounts of 5-FC to enter — synergistic effect.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Imidazoles and Triazoles?
flashcards.back: Triazoles (Fluconazole, Voriconazole, Itraconazole) have less toxicity and fewer drug interactions than Imidazoles (Ketoconazole, Miconazole, Clotrimazole).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of the Azoles?
flashcards.back: Inhibit P450-dependent C14-alpha demethylase, the enzyme responsible for converting Lanosterol to Ergosterol in the fungal cell membrane.

flashcards.front: Why should Ketoconazole not be combined with Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Ketoconazole reduces Ergosterol in the fungal membrane, thereby reducing the efficacy of Amphotericin B, which needs Ergosterol to bind to.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of Fluconazole over Ketoconazole?
flashcards.back: Fluconazole: excellent CSF penetration, does not require gastric acidity for absorption, does not affect glandular hormones, and is excreted renally.

flashcards.front: What is the drug of choice for invasive aspergillosis?
flashcards.back: Voriconazole (has replaced Amphotericin B for this indication).

flashcards.front: What are the hormonal effects of Ketoconazole?
flashcards.back: At high doses it inhibits synthesis of adrenal and sex hormones: gynecomastia, decreased libido, and menstrual irregularities.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Caspofungin?
flashcards.back: Inhibits beta-glucan synthase, thereby blocking synthesis of the fungal cell wall.

flashcards.front: What is the drug of choice for onychomycosis (nail fungus)?
flashcards.back: Terbinafine — inhibits squalene epoxidase, accumulates in skin and nails, treatment duration 3 months.

flashcards.front: What is the most important side effect of Flucytosine?
flashcards.back: Bone marrow depression — caution with radiation and chemotherapy.

flashcards.front: Why does Ketoconazole require an acidic environment for absorption?
flashcards.back: Because it dissolves and is absorbed only in the presence of gastric acidity. H2 blockers, PPIs, and antacids reduce its absorption.

flashcards.front: What is Nystatin and why is it used topically only?
flashcards.back: A polyene similar to Amphotericin B but extremely systemically toxic. Used topically to treat oral candidiasis (thrush) and is not systemically absorbed.

flashcards.front: What are the immediate side effects of Amphotericin B IV?
flashcards.back: Chills and fever (prevented with corticosteroids/NSAIDs), hypotension with hypokalemia, hypersensitivity and anaphylaxis.

flashcards.front: What distinguishes Voriconazole from other Azoles in terms of side effects?
flashcards.back: Visual and auditory hallucinations at elevated concentrations.

flashcards.front: What is the effect of liposomal formulations of Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: They reduce nephrotoxicity and IV infusion toxicity but are expensive.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Binds ergosterol in the fungal membrane → pore formation → ion leakage → fungal killing.

flashcards.front: What are the indications for Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Life-threatening systemic mycoses such as cryptococcal meningitis (DOC).

flashcards.front: What is the most prominent toxicity of Amphotericin B?
flashcards.back: Dose-dependent nephrotoxicity, hypokalemia, and fever/rigors during infusion.

flashcards.front: How can Amphotericin nephrotoxicity be reduced?
flashcards.back: Pre- and post-infusion hydration with normal saline and use of liposomal formulations.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of azoles?
flashcards.back: Inhibit 14-α-demethylase → reduced ergosterol synthesis; inhibit CYP450 (drug interactions).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of echinocandins?
flashcards.back: Inhibit β-glucan synthase → disruption of fungal cell wall (Caspofungin).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Terbinafine?
flashcards.back: Inhibits squalene epoxidase → toxic accumulation of squalene; used for nail fungus.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Griseofulvin?
flashcards.back: Disrupts fungal microtubules; used for dermatophyte infections of skin/hair; enzyme inducer.

mcqs.q: Drug of choice for life-threatening fungal meningitis (cryptococcal meningitis)?
mcqs.explanation: Amphotericin B is the DOC for life-threatening systemic fungal infections and is given intrathecally for fungal meningitis.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Azoles in treating fungal infections?
mcqs.explanation: Azoles inhibit P450-dependent C14-alpha demethylase, preventing the conversion of Lanosterol to Ergosterol.

mcqs.q: Drug of choice for invasive aspergillosis?
mcqs.explanation: Voriconazole has become the DOC for invasive aspergillosis, replacing Amphotericin B for this indication.

mcqs.q: Flucytosine acts inside the fungal cell by:
options: Being converted to 5-FU to inhibit DNA and RNA
mcqs.explanation: 5-FC is converted inside the fungal cell only to 5-FU, which inhibits thymidylate synthase and thereby DNA and RNA.

mcqs.q: A patient is taking Ketoconazole. Which of the following drugs should be avoided concurrently?
mcqs.explanation: Ketoconazole and Amphotericin B should not be combined because Ketoconazole reduces Ergosterol in the fungal membrane, reducing the efficacy of Amphotericin B.

mcqs.q: What is the most important delayed side effect of Amphotericin B?
mcqs.explanation: Nephrotoxicity is the most important delayed effect of Amphotericin B and is dose-dependent.

mcqs.q: What is the pharmacokinetic advantage of Fluconazole compared to Ketoconazole?
options: Absorption is independent of gastric acidity and excellent CSF penetration
mcqs.explanation: Fluconazole is absorbed regardless of gastric acidity, has excellent CSF penetration, and is excreted renally.

mcqs.q: Drug of choice for onychomycosis (nail fungus)?
mcqs.explanation: Terbinafine is the DOC for onychomycosis because it accumulates in skin and nails, has a shorter treatment duration (3 months), and higher efficacy.
Caspofungin (Echinocandin) inhibits beta-glucan synthase, preventing fungal cell wall synthesis.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is correct about Nystatin?
options: Its mechanism differs from Amphotericin B
Used topically only due to severe systemic toxicity
mcqs.explanation: Nystatin is similar to Amphotericin B in mechanism (binds Ergosterol) but is extremely systemically toxic, so it is used topically only for oral candidiasis.

mcqs.q: The most prominent toxicity of Amphotericin:

high_yield.point: Amphotericin B دواء الخيار للفطريات الجهازية المهددة للحياة (التهاب السحايا بالمستخفيات).
Amphotericin B يرتبط بالـ ergosterol → مسامات بالغشاء → تسرّب الأيونات → قاتل للفطر.
سُمّية Amphotericin: كلوية (معتمدة على الجرعة)، نقص بوتاسيوم، حمى/رعشة بالتسريب → اسبق بمحلول ملحي.
Azoles تثبّط CYP450 → تداخلات دوائية كثيرة؛ سُمّية كبدية.
